genre_refs: WPA/Soviet socialist-realism worker poster; 1930s-1940s vintage propaganda illustration style
description: A vertical, AI-generated illustrated propaganda poster in a retro mid-20th-century socialist-realist / WPA style. It depicts an idealized, square-jawed, handsome young white male worker with neatly coiffed reddish-brown hair, gazing heroically off to the upper left. He wears a clean bright-green button-up work shirt with two chest pockets and a brown leather belt. Behind him is a stylized industrial skyline of pale-pink/sepia buildings and tall construction tower cranes against a soft blue cloud-streaked sky, evoking growth and building. Across the very top in large bold blue capital letters is the slogan 'BUILDING THE FUTURE' (partly cropped by the worker's head). A small circular U.S. Department of Labor seal appears in the lower-right corner. The illustration style is smooth, painterly, airbrushed and idealized in the manner of vintage recruitment/labor posters. The propaganda technique is heroic idealization of the skilled blue-collar worker and nostalgic Americana aesthetics to glamorize the trades and the administration's 'Make America Skilled Again' workforce messaging. Target audience: young men, prospective trade/apprenticeship workers, and a general patriotic audience.
